title: telemetry — field classification
description: Data that ships out of the application via OTLP (spans + metrics) OR Monolog sinks (log records). Classified per what appears in span attributes / metric labels / log records.

restricted: academorix.causer_email (only when explicitly enabled + redacted after audit review)
db.statement (query redacted — bind parameters replaced with `?`)
regulated_minor: Any span/log record referencing a minor's user_id must be redacted per COPPA / GDPR Art. 8
regulated_financial: Never emitted: PAN, CVV, expiry, full card number — redactor patterns catch anything matching card-number regex.

notes: Shared redactor pipeline runs BEFORE either export path. Rules configured in telemetry.redact.keys[] and telemetry.redact.patterns[].
IP addresses are treated as personal data under GDPR when combined with other identifiers. Config option telemetry.redact.ip_in_production=true strips http.client_ip. Kept in dev for debugging.
SQL bindings NEVER exported — db.statement is the redacted template only (`SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?`).
Redaction is fail-safe: unknown keys are kept, but if a rule flags a value, the field is replaced with `[REDACTED]`. Better to over-redact than to leak.
